channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 191 replies — 175.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 191 • Updated: 2026-06-11 11:46 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

62340953
>>62340347
>But his timing is fully transparent.


classic boyscout bold faced lie

there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales other than them doing it 4 times per year. often it will be completely different months as years past.

didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol

62340971
>>62340953
>there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales
- always around 7% of total supply annually
- always on Fridays
- always within 3 to 4 month intervals

62342184
>>62340953
>didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol
Those are the transactions I posted that >>62340971 is trying to brush off as "part of the plan"

He's moving the goal post even when this discussion just bolsters my point about the link token price not accurately reflecting network usage. I initially wasn't thinking of the "routine token unlocks" so I'll give you my thanks.

62342243
>>62342184
You're claiming he unlocked right before the 10/10 crash to "capitalize on market movements".

The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval. Like he's done for years.

62342296
>>62342243
Oh no no no, he most certainly did unlock just before the crash. It was part of the panic and it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis.

62342402
>>62342296
>he most certainly did unlock just before the crash
Who said he didn't?

>it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis
It was luck BECAUSE he'd already been selling on a 3-4 month basis for years at that point.

62342424
Reminder you must only say positive things, otherwise you are a shill.

62342440
>>62342424
say something nice about chainlink

62342501
>>62313866
>>62313871
Glad to see some of us are still here... I didn't do the presale but t0 Binance buyer.
It's absolutely grim the state of this project.

62342517
>>62342243
>The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.

yeah they do it once a quarter
this particular one we're talking about just happened to be directly before one of the largest selloffs in industry history

>but duuuuuuuuude he always does it like that

no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months. so no it is not just like previous years.

62342559
>>62342517
>no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months
That's because he's unlocking every 3 to 4 months. Not in the same month every time.
He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.

62342602
>dumps straight on the most shady CEX are "unlocks"
peak doublespeak

62344923
>>62342602
All of Chainlink's tokens go through Binance, including node payments.
Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

62346060
>>62342559
>He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.


not sure why you keep repeating this as if this implies some transparency.

>duuuuuuuuuude just hang out on the blockchain every friday and you'll see it coming

its ok to concede they don't want to be transparent here. if they did they would just post a set schedule.

>>62344923
>Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

no one falls for this outside of the biggest retard boyscouts
where do the coins go?

62346092
>>62346060
Unlocking the same amount annually for years, within the same interval for years, and even around the same time of day for years implies transparency, yes.

>where do the coins go?
among others to the nodes

62346134
link blowz

62349168
Posting in the best LINK thread now. Some good convos. I hope we all make it.

62350386
>>62349168
its actually starting to reverbate with the muh influencers, they actuall read this thread, it's a good thing my goal is reached im happy

item.title: I need financial advice from /bongbiz/, should I prioritise a Stocks ISA or Cash ISA? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I need financial advice from /bongbiz/, should I prioritise a Stocks ISA or Cash ISA?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-11 12:43 UTC

OP

I need financial advice from /bongbiz/, should I prioritise a Stocks ISA or Cash ISA?

Early replies (first 3)

62350281
S&S ISA - Buy SPDR MSCI ACWI and forget.

Pension first for the tax relief though if you believe there will be a Britain worth living in, in 30-40 years and they won't fuck you up by changing the rules and tax around it. (I don't).

62350292
>>62350269
S&S Isa. Set up a standing order to automatically purchase whatever ammount you can afford monthly into a low fees ETF of your choice. Forget about it and come back in 30 years

62350316
bong here

S&S ISA is the only vehicle worth a damn

fuck everything else, and i'm probably getting out of this hellhole at some point anyway

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350320
>>62350269
S&S ISA hands down. Returns on cash ISA's are shit. Like another anons have said in this thread, even investing in safe ETFs will bring you better returns.

Personally I do all my longs through my S&S ISA. Started with 1k back in October, up to 15k now. But I pick my own stocks and don't really touch ETFs.

62350322
Girl

62350326
I told you he is stalking you he is never getting dunkin donuts at this hour. Do fucking love me yet??????? Damn. Buy the dip though knicks in 5. Miss u

62350329
>>62350320
Even if you're peak boomer and think safe ETFs are too risky because ooo shares scawy some providers let you buy bonds and bond heavy funds anyway which can be higher return than cash ISAs and probably even safer than the banks if you have more than the deposit limit.

item.title: How will this affect South Koreas economy? — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How will this affect South Koreas economy?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-11 12:47 UTC

OP

How will this affect South Koreas economy?

Should I go all-in on a SK index fund?

Early replies (first 3)

62350030
>>62349989
I like that he recognizes bald suffering, but the issue is mostly rampant feminism, so effectively, he is an enabler.

62350349
>>62349989
by shaving you no longer have to worry about styling your hair and it's a repellent for nasty and bad women

62350516
>>62350349
>just bend over and let the norwood reaper fuck you in the ass

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350557
in the usa you should be able to pay for finasteride and minoxidil with an HSA. hair is protection from skin cancer.

62350562
>>62350349
You dont look like a 90s skinhead, you skinnyfat onions baldy.

62350565
>>62350557
Almost everywhere in the world generic finasteride options cost like $20-30 for an entire years supply.

Are the pharma companies scamming burgers by overcharging finasteride like they do with most other meds?

item.title: Reminder that XRP bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever incr… — 13 replies — 18.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Reminder that XRP bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever incr…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-11 12:10 UTC

OP

Reminder that XRP bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever increase in value (they just say kaboom or accuse you of owning an equally shitty crypto)

Early replies (first 3)

62345876
>>62345842
KABOOOOOOOM YOU HOLD CHAINLINK OOOOOOOOOOOKOKKKKKKK

62345879
>>62345876
And lo it was as foretold

62345931
>>62345842
Reminder that <literally any cryptocuckshit> bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever increase in value (they just say kaboom or accuse you of owning an equally shitty crypto)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345936
>>62345931
Fair. Monero or privacy crypto could have been world changing, except you can never cash out or buy anything with it

62345958
>>62345936
By design.

62345960
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62345970
most of the so called utility coins have been around for 14-15 years, if they were actually going to do something they would have 10 times already. Even the fucking dotcom boom crashed and recovered in half the time. What else is there to wait for? Clarity Act? Don't make me laugh.

62346026
>>62345970
You could have held Walmart and apple for 20 years before it really took off, 12 years for Amazon…so you’re a retard

62346045
>>62345960
Does nothing for the xrp price

62346051
>>62345960
>30+ partners

62347125
>>62346045
I’ve been holding XRP for years, but no single good news has pumped its prices. Even when XRP got clarity in court, its price only went up measly 40 cents before it dumped back down. You can declare XRP intergalactic bridge currency, but it will still dump or crab. This is because they’re using bots to suppress the price, which has been documented for a long time.

62349844
>>62347125
>hurr xrp doesn't pump on news and is suppressed
You're an idiot.
XRP did a 2x after the lawsuit ended, and Bitcoin even pumped to help it.

62350438
>>62349844
>You're an idiot
>BTC/USDT chart

item.title: I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump. — 19 replies — 9.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump.

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-11 10:47 UTC

OP

I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump. I've lost so much money since then, I can't believe I actually fell for it. I'm still mostly in just BTC but I actually got scammed, feels horrible man.

Early replies (first 3)

62335000
>buying when it's on the news

62335010
>>62335000
trips of wisdom

62335027
>>62334973
First of all ure gambler so u deserve to lose everything. Second of all, you never buy on the news - you invest (youre gambling not investing so this does not concern you) before news happen by using public info and calculating how good or bad news are plausible.
Third of all, next time look THEY WAY THEY SHOOK HANDS retard. Did this sergey cuckoldery look to you like Trump gave a flying shit about him? This was a sell signal if anything

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62335038
>>62334973
>few thousand dollars
>so much money
Pick one

62336812
>>62334973

You should have bought Polkadot

62340148
>>62334973
Chin up lad

62340299
>>62334973
You are waiting on the banks releasing their products ahead of the Clarity Act. There were two laws that needed to be passed before the value of those laws can be demonstrated, Genius was the first. Clarity is next, maybe this month. After that, banks can finally start using smart contracts in production environments and also the Tokenized real world asset market will explode.

If these two markets are everything you hear about in the news and Link's token price is still in the dumps, then you can panic. Link's token price was always suppressed to keep it out of the top 10. They're not going to let it rip till they have to since they don't scrubs like us owning a share of their financial network.

62341169
Buy the rumor.
Sell the news.

62342097
>>62334973
brock > misty

62342174
>>62340299
You say that, but we could buy less if the price was higher

62342183
>>62340299
this is larpcope to keep bagholders from killing themselves

62344770
>>62342174
this

>>62342183
and this

/biz/ can't handle being wrong about link, and the delusion will only spiral deeper

62344773
>>62342097
Disagree. Brock has a major type weakness against Misty.

62346469
>>62342174
>You say that, but we could buy less if the price was higher

This presumes people understand the Link token. The banking world and VCs are aware that a bunch of racist frogs are all obsessed with the project and they overlook such a thing. The common masses, on the other hand, they only pay attention to the top 10. It's impossible to shakeout every frog, but they're doing a great enough job keep Link under the radar otherwise and keeping people too demoralized to even recommend the project as an investment to others.

62346485
>>62346469
>da elites don't want a bunch of anons to make 3 million dollars!
holy shit you cuckolds are really insane lol

62346620
>>62346469
Baggies will really stare you dead in the eye and parrot that tired fucking line about the elites suppressing price on purpose to shake them out as boomers and normie retards are getting spoonfed in semis and AI. Evil disingenuous nigger. I really can't wait for North Korea to target CLL with Mythos, I really can't.

62346674
>>62346620
How else do you explain the convenient BTC dumps every time there's big news about Chainlink? How do you justify the DTCC saying they're launching their own chain with Link as the infrastructure and the price doesn't move? The DTCC processes over a quadrillion dollars in transactions a year. How else do you explain this?

62347714
>>62334973
I bought my first 2k LINK immediately after seeing an American Psycho business card meme about it. I had no idea what it was and hadn't even heard of Ethereum at that point, it just resonated with me for some reason.
Then after reading the white paper and doing some basic research I bought another 10k or so.

62347893
>>62346674
>Hehe we're going to overthrow the entire financial system with our shitcoin
>AIEEEEE WHY IS OUR SHITCOIN'S PRICE ACTION STILL TIED TO BITCOIN
Reality:
>None of the infinite partnerships headlines accrue any value to the token whatsoever.
>The "strategic reserve" has gone from a novelty to the main carrot dangling in a scant few months and when that dribbles to 0 they will invent another one.
>CLL has zero legal obligation to make any of you wealthy or give you legal rights as a tokenholder.
>You are not shareholders.
>There is nothing stopping a tradfi entity from acquiring CLL and leaving every tokenholder in the dust and even the most hardcore goylink'd shill on Xitter will tell you the same

item.title: My portfolio is getting RAPED by the orange nigger right now. — 10 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
My portfolio is getting RAPED by the orange nigger right now.

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-11 11:43 UTC

OP

My portfolio is getting RAPED by the orange nigger right now. I have no liquidity left

Early replies (first 3)

62348326
>>62348306
buy high quality dividend players so next time you get ____ed you have some dividends to fall back on. also watch your language mister

62348343
>>62348326
Nigger I own MEMECOINS

62348384
>>62348306
I voted for this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62348402
>>62348343
Well you are a nigger retard.

Come on, honestly, who the fuck do you think is buying memes who has serious capital? Any moves in memes is just smoke and mirrors.

62348424
>>62348306
all my tech stocks that were pumping the past few months have had a brutal past week. should I sell everything? Im scared. Maybe it will go back to normal once this stupid space x shit is over with? no doubt normies are selling everything right now because they have the retarded illusion that theyre going to get rich off space x ipo.

62349065
>>62348424
Normies don't have money and can't move markets like you think they can. It's whales creating retail bait so normies can buy the tippity top and then whales yank the carpet out from under the norms.

62349091
how? isn't the stock market booming.

62349297
>>62348306
You should have invested in rape. You really didn't think a pro-rape president wouldn't implement policies to help the rape industry?

62350372
>>62348402
Suck my balls cuck
I made more money from memes than your mother does working the street corner

62350377
stock market is on sale, time to buy more

It is dumping again!

62350544
scamming fuck. reset the count to it's true value you arrogant shit

>>62349644
KABOOOOOOOM

No one cares about your token.

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 57 replies — 32.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-06-11 11:07 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345054
>>62340066
What's happening in October?

62345074
>>62345048
yea but you could have bought the 50% discount with some of the profits instead of fresh shekels

62345096
>>62345074
Smart guy

62345100
>>62345001
How can we stake btc?

62345127
hold till $1M

62345299
>>62345054
were supposed to bottom, 4 year cycle or some shit.

62345600
>>62345100
You haven't heard of Babylon? Native BTC staking already exists there. DYOR anon.

62345604
>>62339510
The real challenge is whether August actually lets you buy lower. Markets have a habit of humbling everyone's timelines.

62345772
>>62345299
Is 4year cycle even real?

62345775
>>62345127
40 years from now

62345776
>>62339328
I sold a small amount at 110k last year, bought a car. Unironically I felt bad once it started pumping back to 120k. Now my car has holded it's value better than BTC.

62345783
>>62345776
Car price depreciated. Nothing special

62345787
>>62341032
I just want x1000 . Utility is nothing

62345800
>>62345600
centralised services doesn't really give me a good impression

62345806
When to sell?

62345827
>>62339328
>Btc
>Eth
>Hbar
>Neo
>Etc
>Kava
>Rose
>Inj

62346229
>>62344890
Yep. Make sure you ignore the fiat value. If it falls to zero, BTC is a new way of finance independent from the outdated monetary system. Be grateful you are part of the revolution. I too will soon enter BTC paradise. Any day now...

62346754
>>62345800
Anon, where did I say anything about centralized services? You're arguing against something I never said. Read up on their TBVs and maybe it'll click.

62346765
>>62339730
That's understandable. It's hard to judge past decisions based on outcomes we couldn't have known at the time.

62349025
>>62346229
Btc will never go to zero. Stop smoking

62349066
>>62345827
oldie bags but gold . Hodl

62349435
>>62346754
Onchain is the game

62349439
>>62345806
Depends on you lmao

62349795
>>62339328
If I had sold to pay off debt, buy a house, or improve my life, I wouldn't regret it. You can't eat unrealized gains.

62350085
>>62345787
go for shitcoins like memecoins on solana

62350154
>>62339328

Buy Polkadot

https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/03/establishment-of-the-strategic-bitcoin-reserve-and-united-states-digital-asset-stockpile/

62350283
>>62349435
Yeah. Going on-chain is one thing. Now let's see if the infrastructure can back it up. The public testnet on Aave V4 will speak for itself.

62350284
>>62350154
What happened to polkadot?

62350290
>>62345050

62350315
>>62350290
Ethereum is king

item.title: BTC — 14 replies — 28.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-11 12:23 UTC

OP

Did anyone figure out why the value seems to be cyclical?
Whats the best exchange and wallet for a new to investing anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62347134
>>62347031
so they can continuously pump and dump and pretend it's simply "natural" and "mathematical" and whatever other bullshit marketing they do

62347139
>>62347031
>cycles
Human psychology

>exchange
Kraken

>wallet
Your memory, but I’ll let other people shill their products below

62347162
>>62347139
He's new to it. Start with the Bluewallet app on your phone, look for more secure alternatives as you go.

https://bitcoin.org/en/choose-your-wallet

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62347465
Thank you for your helpful responses frens

62347618
>>62347031
Its a self fulfilling prophecy at this point but it started because of the halving.

62347821
>>62347031
>Did anyone figure out why the value seems to be cyclical?
each 4 years there is a halving

>Whats the best exchange and wallet for a new to investing anon?
4chin censors the reply, probably site is run by some retards
b. core
change b. to full name of the coin
tripple check sources before install

source: og here

62347830
>>62347031

62348122
>>62347821
So the "b . Org" is the wrong one?

I hate how predatory search engines are now that we are the product

62350049
>>62348122
bitcoin core is the real program, og

bitcoin core dot org is the site
but also/better check github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin

62350055
>>62348122
>>62350049
bitcoin.org is the original site, yes, it has a list of wallets, including the bitcoincore main wallet

to get rich/avoid getting poor:
- keep backups
- doublecheck backups
- make more backups
- document how you restore and use backups, so if you give up on btc and find this back in 10 years you can recover it still
- more backups, in case of a fire

- do NOT keep money on exchange, withdraw
- do NOT move all money at once. go slow. send, wait hours, verify, continue
- just buy weekly or "daily" (daily cost averaging). buy more if it falls a lot
- never sell it all

62350264
>>62350055
That is very helpful, thank you :)

62350298
>>62350055
checked && self custody is for retards.

62350304
>>62347139
>Human psychology
Jewish psychology

62350478
>>62350264
cheers

also avoid altcoins. they don't plug into a printer even

item.title: Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. — 34 replies — 11.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-06-11 09:53 UTC

OP

Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. Look at this poster who copies and pastes an entire thread just to shit it up and make fun of XRP holders: >>62338460

The funny thing? He forgot to remove the (You) when he copied and pasted the whole thing. But you can see his ID is different.

So my best guess is it's probably a single poster who constantly switches id to force this shitty meme. Probably a chainlink bagholder.

Early replies (first 3)

62339778
Imagine caring this much, holy shit. Adult bully victim no doubt

62339781
>>62339778
Lol of course I don't care so much but it goes to show how pathetic those link tards are trying to force that unfunny boom-xrp meme. The guy thinks if he switches id it looks like more people are in on it but that retard forgot to remove the (You) from his own post.

You are just butthurt you got called out hard and i'm gonna show this to everyone on /biz/ so they know who is really responsible for forcing that dumb meme.

62339829
>>62339778
idk bout that... xrp apparently lives rent free in linkbaggies minds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62340175
>>62339844
Xrp hasn't reclaimed it's all time high after a whole decade. If xrp baggies didn't sell they have blown their chance of limiting their losses and buying something useful

Kaboom

62340182
>>62339768
Its biepum the yeet node operator
Give him a break, chainlink shilling and xrp fudding is his personality

62340345
>>62339768
so fxcking bullish

62340350
Boooomers and peelers won

62340365
>>62339768
>Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.
GIGABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62340374
>>62339768
Why would anyone buy Chainlink or xrp I don't understand. Crypto is there to buy drugs online. Can you buy drugs online with Chainlink ? No. Can you buy drugs online with xrp? No. Case closed.

62340393
>>62339768
I don't hold link either tho
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62340516
everybody hates u dumb stinks

62340978
>>62339781
>words words words
It's funny how you're immediately identifiable by the spergy seethe that radiates from your posts.

Even funnier, >>62339844 posting the madmen meme.
>"We don't think about Linkies at all!!"
>Op literally creates a thread ranting about Chainlink because some other random thread that doesn't mention Chainlink could have been made by a Linkie

62340989
>>62340978
>TLDR linkjeet seethe

didnt read lol

62340992
BOOOOOOOOOOM

BULLISH AT XRP

62341232
Bumping this bullsih thread
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62342495
>>62339768
>My retarded shitposts made someone so mad he made an expose thread on me
FXCKING BULLISH KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62343674
>>62339768
>>62339781
>>62339835
>>62340989
>linkcucks and xrpaypigs having a retarded biz slapfight as they both go to 0

62344317
KABOOOOOOOOM>>62339768

62344795
>>62339781
This. I don't hold either but the link spam is so fucking stupid and annoying. They got decimated in a war length campaign of threads and saw zero wins and now it is over and over with this same shitty failed meme that ironically got funny when XRP posters just did it back to them to mock them. I have zero dog in this race but XRP is the clear winner in the messaging war.

62344908
>>62344795
>t. the same person a different id
manufacturing consensus on a dead board is pretty cringe desu senpai

62345267
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
link

62345354
Except XRP is actually being used

62347256
KABOOOOOOOOM

62347334
>>62344795
BOOOOOOOOOOM YOU HOLD XRP

62347434
>>62339781
>”I don’t care”
>paragraph about how much he cares

62347439
>>62339768
Both LINK and XRP are gay

62348331
I mean…it’s pretty hard not to cope and seethe with pictures like this

62348340
>>62348331
Must....not......boom...must...not.......
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
BULLISH AT THE TICKER XRP

62349668
>>62344908
You are incapable of conceiving of someone who just calls out your hypocrisy and annoying shilling. This is why you are how you are and you are like this because you do this. It is circular and self-feeding and swallowing. You are in a vortex, accusing me of doing exactly what you are in an even more retarded way than you are even accusing me of doing.
>>62347334
See the above reply. Same applies to you.

62349808
>>62349668
You are the verbose xrp shill though. You made this >>62349672 reply to the xrp general minutes after this one here in the same verbose style.

62349917
>>>62349808
Mentally ill VPN linkbaggie

62350205
>>62349808
It is indeed extremely obvious whenever he posts. Lately he is always talking about "muh XRP kept it's gains from the Trump pump!" yet XRP is down 70% from the ATH it made during said pump. Insanely delusional.

item.title: So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. — 14 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo.

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-11 11:30 UTC

OP

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. How the fuck is it legal for them to destroy so much shareholder value? I WANT MY FUCKING MONEY BACK NOW!!

Early replies (first 3)

62348099
>>62348037
You did not lose anything, shoe shine boy. change your diapers and calm the FUCK down. Because you didn't sell yet. Right?

62348149
Better price to buy m8. You don't believe in the business anymore or somethin

62348170
If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead, I don't know what to tell you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62348177
>>62348037
nintendo is dead. the new pokemon games all have a super weird art style

62348263
>>62348170
I couldn't tell, because I stopped paying attention (and let it die i guess)

Is there anything these days like Minecraft Halloween update, the summer of dayz, or starceaft brood war Use Map Settings maps?

62348278
>>62348037

Are you the same retard that asked about buying them after an entire thread told you they were cooked? I remember giving clear examples of why this company is dogshit, specifically not owning Pokémon 100%. They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son. Sounds like you are a braindead tendie lie the rest.

62349809
>>62348099
I would have lost more if I hadn't sold, retard.

62349911
>>62348037
I FUCKING told end of last year that Nintendo was in a bubble. Yes, it's in the archives, likely with either Xenoblade pics or Rosalina pics.
>Muh P/E is useless
It isn't. As many retards will soon learn again for lot of shit they buy at overpriced prices.
Now it's at a more reasonable price, still elevated, but low enough that I'm regularly buying.

62349918
>>62349911
nintebdo movies will each make a bil

62349956
the next ubisoft

62349957
>>62348037
you could have made 100k scalping pokemon cards, wtf are you doing OP

62349964
>>62348037
Japan as a society is super conservative and stuck in its way to a retarded degree, what do you expect from the land of fax machines and archipelago syndrome?

62350244
>>62349911
What stocks options are you buying bro?

62350352
>>62348037
>all good games are made by indie developers
why would you bet on any of the big companies? they are creatively bankrupt bloated bureaucracies

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 106 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 106 • Updated: 2026-06-11 00:40 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62329055
>>62309503
intrinsic value doesn't exist
bitcoin is going to dozens of millions but for now the end of the bear cycle is not over and the bottom will likely be end of year

62329158
>>62309515
Buy the fucking dip and stake the little you can, you can never catch the bottom. If you panic sell, it will start pumping.

62329178
I hope this thread can survive another week or two, so we can laugh at OP when Bitcoin dumps to $50k

62329324
>>62329055
There is no bear cycle, it is just retards fire selling so they have free cash to buy into SpaceX IPO and end up inevitably rugged.

The BUYROG will win in the end. I hope this thread stays up until the end od the month, when BTC is at 75k and trending upwards because S&P refused to steal people's retirement to buy the latest AI scam.

62329342
>>62329324
Man, I hope you're right
It's been 8 straight months of dumping, need to see some green for a change

62329345
>>62329324
>52% drop over the last 8 months, with lower and lower bottoms over time
>"There is no bear cycle"

62329476
>>62329342
Serious, dude, just ignore these people (bear posters are just /smg/ bots and /pmg/ twofaced assholes, go tend to your garden, and come back in a few weeks.

The last time it was the same shit. The BUYROG called the bottom, they all laughed and posted bears, then the bottom was kept, and BTC rallied to near 80k before the SpaceX IPO prepping started.

We are frankly better off without these people. BTC is literally outperforming silver and 90% of crypto right now, yet they act like this is the end of crypto (it is not).

The idea of crypto is what matters, and they hate and fear this idea because they will lose, as they have lost many times before. Just zoom out the price charts.

This is not buy x sell, it is good x evil, and we are better off without retards buying into BTC so they can pump and dump.

62329478
>>62309503
/biz/ tricked me into waiting for a lower dip during the last bottom. I'm going to DCA now

62329970
>>62309503
Dunno m8 at my local groxery store they dib't accept bitcoin. Where xan you buy groceries, other than your cherrypicked example?

62331787
>>62329478
/biz/ always tells you to buy the dip. Then the dip comes, and they tell you not to buy it.

62331939
Brought to you by Epstein and Co.: Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value!

62333686
>>62309503
I bought the rally (my first cycle) and the dips/bottom. waiting on the good days now

62333871
>>62309503
30k

62334072
63K REJECTED

62334365
>>62309503
>65K is the bottom
But it went lower anon?

62334381
>>62329970
you can buy anything you want with a coinbase card, what're you on about geez?

62336135
THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING AT ALL OF THE BEAR SHILLS

YOU LOST. YOU SOLD THE BOTTOM

THE BUYROG WINS AGAIN

BEARSBANE

62336205
>>62309503
At least until it dips again. 26K is the real bottom, screen cap this.

62336221
>>62336205
It is never going 55k. I have no idea why PM metal shills (most of the bear is PM fags, we tested it) watch silver get ended by the Fed, and decide to lash out trolling people's financial lives.

I am glad the BUYROG convinced some people to not miss yet another dip.

62336238
>>62336135
>THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING
>still below the 65k "bottom" of OP

62337880
>>62336238
YOU LOST

62340948
Bear bros, our response? The buyrog keeps calling it right.

62341211
>>62340948
Sorry, I can’t hear anything with the noise of all these coins crashing at the same time.

62341507
>>62341211
just bought $20k MSTY

62342143
Your conviction is admirable, but not warranted.

62344662
Bobobros, it’s not really crashing anymore…

62344689
>>62309503
Bitcoin brought about crypto, but it has limited growth potential at this point due to both mcap and being so centralized. Decentralized coins will keep growing, and some have grown even while everything else tanks, like Nerva/XNV.

62344772
>>62344689
Explain how btc is centralized.

62348813
>>62344772
You need a centralized exchange to buy and sell your digital pixels so the government can tax and keep track of your transactions.

62348845
>>62348813
Yes, you simply can't buy it with cash.
